def: Money you don't spend now so you can use it in the future.
tip: Even RM1 a day can help build strong saving habits over time.

def: Money you borrow and must pay back later, usually with interest.
tip: Only borrow what you can comfortably repay each month.

def: A plan that tells your money where to go before you spend it.
tip: Use the 50/30/20 rule as a guide and review your budget monthly. Tip: Use EPF's Belanjawanku app!

def: Taking money out of your bank account or ATM.
tip: Avoid withdrawing more cash than needed to control unnecessary spending.

def: A card that lets you borrow money to make purchases and repay later.
tip: Spend only what you can repay fully each month to avoid high interest charges.

def: Using money to buy things that can increase in value over time.
tip: Start small and invest regularly for long-term growth.

def: When prices rise, and your money buys less than before.
tip: Plan ahead and build savings to handle rising costs.

def: Money saved for unexpected expenses like emergencies, repairs, or urgent needs.
tip: Aim for 3–6 months of essential expenses so you have a financial buffer when life surprises you.

def: A share of profits paid from savings or investments.
tip: Reinvest your dividends or let them grow to strengthen your long-term savings.

def: Financial protection that helps cover unexpected costs such as medical emergencies, accidents, or damage.
tip: Review your coverage regularly to ensure it matches your needs and life stage. Tip: Explore EPF's Member Protection Plan (i-Lindung) options.

def: A rating that shows how responsibly you manage debt and repayments.
tip: Pay bills and loans on time to keep a good score.

hint: You gain more interest on existing interest of your savings
def: When your money earns returns, and those returns also start earning returns over time.
tip: Start early and save regularly to maximise growth.

def: The process of managing loans and repayments responsibly to avoid excessive debt.
tip: Keep track of repayment dates and avoid borrowing beyond your ability to repay.
